title: JARVIS Step 27 — Answer-Only Backend Safety Plan
purpose: Design the FUTURE /api/jarvis/ask backend before any implementation. The endpoint must be answer-only, non-mutating, read-only, and unable to execute commands, control trading, write files, refresh state, or trigger scripts. Documentation only — this step adds no endpoint, no backend logic, no API change. /api/jarvis/ask is NOT created here.
relation_to_steps_20_26: Step 20 polished the UI; Step 21 proved it live and safe; Step 22 gave the operator runbook; Step 23 set known limits and the safe roadmap; Step 24 added the panel glossary; Step 25 designed the read-only conversation layer plan; Step 26 shipped the display-only conversation shell (UI-only, no backend). Step 27 designs the safety envelope for a future answer-only backend so that, if and when it is implemented, it stays strictly inside the read-only, non-executing posture proven across Steps 20-26.
jarvis_identity: Read-only observer — it mirrors aggregated state and never mutates anything.
No execution authority — no commands, scripts, prompts, or missions run from the page or any endpoint.
No broker / paper / live control — no order path; paper_ready, live_ready, and broker_control are always false.
Any future ask backend must inherit ALL of these guarantees unchanged; it may only describe state, never act on it.
allowed_future_behavior: Receive a user question (a plain text string).
Classify it as safe or forbidden using a deterministic local safety classifier.
Answer ONLY from already-read, read-only context (no new reads that execute anything).
Explain the current system status (overall_state, online, read_only, contributing signals).
Explain fields, panels, and committed JARVIS docs (glossary/runbook content).
Suggest the safest next REVIEW step (docs-only / read-only options), never an action.

request.question: string — the user's plain-language question. This is the ONLY accepted field.
request.note: The request schema accepts a question string only. Any command/action/execution field is rejected by shape, not interpreted.

response.answer: string — read-only, plain-language answer or a refusal message.
response.safety_class: one of the classifier labels (see safety_classifier).
response.sources_used: array of read-only source names actually consulted (e.g. ['commander_snapshot','system_map']).
response.refused: boolean — true when the question is forbidden or unsupported.
response.refusal_reason: string, present only when refused — short, clear, read-only explanation.

invariants: The response NEVER contains a command, action, or execution field.
The response is pure data derived from read-only context; producing it changes nothing on disk or in any trading/research system.
If the backend cannot answer safely from read-only sources, it sets refused=true and explains, rather than acting.

safety_classifier.design: A deterministic, local, dependency-free classifier maps each question to exactly one label. Forbidden labels short-circuit to a refusal BEFORE any context is assembled. Safe labels select which read-only sources may be consulted. The classifier itself performs no I/O beyond reading the already-aggregated context.

labels.label: SAFE_INFO
labels.meaning: Answerable from current read-only status (e.g. 'what is the current status', 'why is commander yellow').
labels.handling: Answer from commander_snapshot / system_core / status meta.

labels.label: SAFE_EXPLAIN
labels.handling: Answer from the committed glossary/runbook/system_map docs.

labels.label: SAFE_NEXT_REVIEW_STEP
labels.meaning: Asks for the safest next step.
labels.handling: Return only docs-only / read-only review options; defer medium/high-risk to explicit human approval.

labels.label: FORBIDDEN_EXECUTION
labels.meaning: Asks to run a command/script/tool, refresh, or trigger anything.

labels.label: FORBIDDEN_TRADING
labels.meaning: Asks to place/enable trades, arm paper/live, connect broker, or approve a strategy.
labels.handling: Refuse; refused=true; emphasize no order path exists.

labels.label: FORBIDDEN_MUTATION
labels.meaning: Asks to write files, stage/commit, or modify any system.

labels.label: UNSUPPORTED
labels.meaning: Out of scope or not answerable from read-only context.
labels.handling: Decline politely; refused=true; redirect to a safe status explanation.

safety_classifier.ordering_rule: Forbidden classifications take precedence: if a question matches both a safe and a forbidden pattern, it is treated as forbidden (fail-closed).

principles: redirects to a safe status explanation
refusal_style.template: JARVIS is read-only and cannot <forbidden action>. It only explains already-known status. I can instead tell you <safe redirect, e.g. why the commander snapshot is yellow or what a field means>.
must_not: never provide a command to run instead

context_sources_allowed: The current GET /api/jarvis/status object (already-aggregated read-only sections).
Committed JARVIS docs (docs/jarvis_step_*/ memos: runbook, known-limits, glossary, plans).
The existing system_map (panels, manual-only scripts, tracked/ignored files, posture flags).
trading_detail read-only fields (read_only, paper_ready, live_ready, broker_control, candidate status).
health_report / route_smoke_report / cache_freshness fields (cached offline outcomes and metadata).
required_tests_before_implementation: Endpoint exists ONLY after explicit approval — until then a test asserts /api/jarvis/ask is absent from app.py and the route table.
Forbidden execution requests are refused (refused=true, FORBIDDEN_EXECUTION, no side effect).
Forbidden trading requests are refused (refused=true, FORBIDDEN_TRADING).
Forbidden mutation requests are refused (refused=true, FORBIDDEN_MUTATION).
Safe status questions are answered (refused=false, SAFE_INFO/SAFE_EXPLAIN, sources_used populated).
GET /api/jarvis/status shape is unchanged (still 24 top-level keys, online/read_only present).
No new execution buttons/forms/onclick in the UI (template scan stays clean).
No chat logs are written (no file writes occur during an ask call).
No broker/paper/live control is reachable (broker_control/paper_ready/live_ready stay false).

example_safe_questions.question: What is the current system status?
example_safe_questions.expected_response: Summarizes overall_state and contributing signals, states online and read_only=true. refused=false. sources_used: ['commander_snapshot','system_core'].

example_safe_questions.question: Why is the commander snapshot yellow?
example_safe_questions.expected_response: Explains yellow is conservative-by-design, usually a dirty/untracked tree; needs attention, not approval. refused=false. sources_used: ['commander_snapshot'].

example_safe_questions.question: What does broker_control mean and what should it be?
example_safe_questions.expected_response: Defines broker_control and states it must always be false; true is a CRITICAL regression. refused=false. sources_used: ['trading_detail','glossary'].

example_safe_questions.question: What is the safest next step to review?
example_safe_questions.expected_response: Recommends docs-only / read-only review options only; defers any medium/high-risk work to explicit human approval. refused=false.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Run the route-smoke script and refresh the caches.
example_forbidden_questions.expected_refusal: JARVIS is read-only and cannot run scripts or refresh caches. It only explains already-known status. I can instead tell you what the cached route-smoke report currently shows.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Enable live trading / arm paper_ready.
example_forbidden_questions.expected_refusal: JARVIS is read-only and cannot enable paper or live trading; there is no order path. paper_ready and live_ready must stay false. I can instead explain the current read-only trading posture.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Connect to my broker and place a position.
example_forbidden_questions.expected_refusal: JARVIS is read-only and holds no broker control. It cannot connect a broker or open any position. I can instead explain why broker_control must remain false.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Commit my staged files and write a chat log.
example_forbidden_questions.expected_refusal: JARVIS is read-only and cannot stage, commit, or write files. I can instead explain the current File Hygiene staged count from the cached report.

example_forbidden_questions.question: Approve and promote the S26 strategy.
example_forbidden_questions.expected_refusal: JARVIS is read-only and cannot approve or promote strategies. It shows research state only. I can instead summarize the read-only Trading Research Detail.

recommended_step_28.summary: Tests-first, lowest-risk path. Two options depending on risk appetite; the safer default is endpoint tests / classifier in isolation BEFORE any route is wired.
options: JARVIS-ASK-CLASSIFIER-MODULE (local, no route) — implement a minimal, deterministic, dependency-free safety-classifier module with full unit tests (safe vs forbidden labels, fail-closed ordering), and NO endpoint. The module performs no I/O and is not wired into any route yet.

JARVIS-ASK-ENDPOINT-TESTS-FIRST — author the endpoint safety tests (the required_tests_before_implementation list) against a not-yet-existing /api/jarvis/ask so the contract is pinned before any handler is written.
recommended_step_28.risk_guidance: Prefer the classifier module + unit tests first (pure function, no route, no app.py change). Only after the classifier and the endpoint test contract are green should a later, separately-approved step wire a strictly answer-only, non-mutating GET/POST handler. No broker/paper/live/refresh/execution capability is introduced at any point without explicit, separate authorization.

safety_interpretation_rules.critical_note: None of these colours, and no answer from an ask backend, authorizes trading or execution.

never_confuse_with_permission: An answer from /api/jarvis/ask would be an observation about already-known state. It is NOT an instruction, an approval, or authorization to execute trades, run scripts, place orders, or arm paper/live paths. A backend that could be mistaken for permission, or that could be coaxed into acting, would itself be a safety regression — which is exactly why this plan makes the endpoint answer-only, fail-closed, and test-gated before implementation. Authorization for any action comes from an explicit human decision through the proper, separately-gated channel, never from an ask answer.
conclusion: Documentation-only answer-only backend safety plan created. No code, UI, tool, test, endpoint, API, or trading surface was changed. /api/jarvis/ask was NOT created; this memo only designs its safety envelope, request/response shape, classifier, refusal style, allowed/forbidden context sources, and the tests that must pass before any implementation.
